title: Recipe Pattern
summary: The Recipe Pattern gives the LLM a goal and partial steps, then asks it to fill in the missing steps to create a complete 'recipe.' You provide what you know (start point, end goal, some intermediate steps) and the LLM fills in the gaps. This is useful when you know part of a process but need help completing it — like knowing your trip's start and end points but needing the LLM to suggest stops along the way. The pattern leverages the LLM's knowledge of common processes and sequences.

key_concepts.definition: A prompt pattern where you provide a goal and partial steps, asking the LLM to fill in the missing steps to create a complete process or sequence.
key_concepts.why_it_matters: When you know part of a solution but not all of it, this pattern gets the LLM to complete the gaps using its knowledge of similar processes.

key_concepts.definition: The LLM's ability to identify missing steps in a partial process and generate appropriate intermediate steps to reach the goal.
key_concepts.why_it_matters: This turns partial knowledge into complete solutions — you provide the framework, the LLM provides the details.

patterns.name: Recipe Pattern
patterns.intent: Get a complete set of steps for a process when you know some steps but not all of them.
patterns.template: I will tell you my {start/goal} and {end/goal}. You will provide a complete list of {steps/stops/items} including {what to fill in between}.
patterns.example_prompt: I will tell you my start and end destination and you will provide a complete list of stops for me, including places to stop between my start and destination. Start: Nashville, TN. End: Denver, CO.
patterns.when_to_use: When you know the start and end of a process but need help with the intermediate steps. Useful for trip planning, project planning, process design, or any sequential task.

techniques: Clearly state what you know (start, end, some intermediate steps) and what you need filled in.
Specify the level of detail you want in the complete recipe.
You can provide constraints: 'only include stops with good restaurants' or 'steps must be under 30 minutes each.'
The LLM uses its training data knowledge of similar processes to fill gaps.
Combine with the Flipped Interaction Pattern if the LLM needs more information to fill gaps effectively.

quiz_questions.question: What is the core idea of the Recipe Pattern?
options: A. Getting cooking recipes from the LLM
B. Providing partial steps of a process and asking the LLM to fill in the missing steps to create a complete sequence
C. Having the LLM follow a recipe you provide
D. Breaking a task into exactly 3 steps
quiz_questions.explanation: The Recipe Pattern is about gap-filling: you provide what you know (start, end, some steps) and the LLM completes the process by adding the missing intermediate steps.

quiz_questions.question: In the trip planning example, what does the user provide and what does the LLM generate?
options: A. User provides the route; LLM provides the car
B. User provides start and end destinations; LLM generates a complete list of stops between them
C. User provides all stops; LLM optimizes the route
D. User provides budget; LLM plans the entire trip
quiz_questions.explanation: The user provides the known endpoints (Nashville to Denver) and the LLM fills in the gaps with suggested stops along the way, creating a complete trip plan.

quiz_questions.question: How does the Recipe Pattern differ from the Cognitive Verifier Pattern?
options: B. Recipe Pattern fills in missing steps of a known process; Cognitive Verifier breaks a question into sub-questions for better reasoning
C. Recipe Pattern is for cooking; Cognitive Verifier is for research
quiz_questions.explanation: Recipe Pattern: you know some steps, LLM fills gaps in a sequence. Cognitive Verifier: you have a question, LLM generates sub-questions to answer it more thoroughly. Different purposes.

raw_examples: I will tell you my start and end destination and you will provide a complete list of stops for me, including places to stop between my start and destination. Start: Nashville, TN. End: Denver, CO.
